project.title: Reanalysis of a synthetic cross-linked peptide library with OpenPepXL for benchmarking purposes
project.projectDescription: Reanalysis of a synthetic crosslinked peptide library dataset. The three replicates of the XL-MS experiment with the non-cleavable cross-linker DSS from the dataset published in Beveridge et al., Nature Commun., 2020 (PRIDE project PXD014337) were searched with the XL-MS identification tool OpenPepXL for benchmarking purposes.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The synthetic peptide library was crosslinked using DSS as described in Beveridge et al., Nature Commun., 2020 (PRIDE project PXD014337).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The .RAW files of all datasets were converted into mzML and MGF files using MSConvertGUI from the ProteoWizard toolkit version 3.0.10577. Reversed sequence decoy protein databases were generated from the target protein databases using the TOPP tool DecoyDatabase from OpenMS 2.5. OpenPepXL 1.2 with the TOPP tool XFDR for False Discovery Rate (FDR) estimation were used to identify cross-links. The search parameters were: precursor tolerance = 5 ppm, fragment tolerance = 20ppm, max. number of missed cleavages = 3, enzyme = Trypsin, min. peptide length = 5, fixed modification = Carbamidomethyl (C), variable modification = Oxidation (M), max. variable modifications per peptide = 2, charges = +2 to +8 , cross-linker = DSS, cross-link mass = 138.0680796, mono-link masses = 156.07864431, linked residues = K. Additional filtering and post-processing was partly done with the TOPP tool IDFilter and TextExporter. The MzIdentML files (*.mzid) contain the complete, unfiltered search results. The tables (*.tsv) contain the final filtered results at 5% (*_fdr5.tsv) and 1% (*_fdr1.tsv) FDR, with removed decoys and mono-links, and cross-links made unique by only keeping the highest scoring CSM for each.
